# After 13 Years of Infertility, Veteran Bay Area TCM Doctors Identify Two Major Causes

**Infertility specialist Dr. Fan:** Poor lifestyle and environmental habits are major fertility killers

## Introduction
Modern infertility is closely tied to whether one’s lifestyle is healthy. In many cases, infertility is caused not only by physical conditions, but also by irregular daily routines, unhealthy eating habits, and even dressing in ways that are unsuitable for the weather.

One infertility case from the Bay Area involved the wife of a government employee, who had been unable to conceive for 13 years. After receiving treatment and lifestyle guidance from veteran physicians at **California Kingsfoil Traditional Chinese Medicine Clinic**, she finally became pregnant.

## Excessive Meat Consumption and Late Nights
The couple’s lifestyle issues were reflected in every aspect of their daily lives and ran counter to traditional healthy routines.

Their diet included excessive amounts of meat, while their sleep schedule involved staying up past midnight and skipping breakfast, eventually disrupting their physical balance.

## Dressing for Fashion Led to Coldness
Modern clothing choices that ignore weather conditions can negatively affect the body. In this case, prolonged exposure to cold due to light clothing contributed to uterine coldness and infertility.

## Restoring Healthy Habits Through Treatment
The clinic corrected the couple’s routines by reducing meat intake, establishing regular meals, improving sleep habits, and using acupuncture and herbal medicine to restore reproductive health and hormonal balance.

## Pregnant With Twins Three Months Later
After treatment, Mrs. Huang’s condition improved significantly. Within a few months, her menstrual cycle returned, and she successfully conceived twins.
